# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### October 20, 2020

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript of the meeting. They may contain inaccuracies due to transcription errors. For official minutes, please consult the district's records.

---

## Call to Order
- Meeting called to order at **5:59 p.m.** by Board President Datren Williams.
- A quorum was confirmed present.
- Trustee Kia led the invocation and Pledge of Allegiance, sharing a passage from Galatians 6:9 as encouragement.

---

## Awards and Recognitions

### 2A — Texas PTA Grant Presentation
- **Susie Kennan**, President of the Texas PTA, presented Conroe ISD with a **$279,200 check** for the purchase of **three clean-running school buses** through the Clean School Bus Grant program, in partnership with the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ).
- Executive Director of Operations **Chris McCord** introduced the presentation.
- The grant was made possible because **Bonnie Wilkinson Elementary** has a PTA, making the district eligible.
- Principal **Melinda Stewart** and volunteer leader **Candace Armstrong** from Bonnie Wilkinson were recognized.
- Kennan noted Texas PTA has paid out over **$2 million** in clean school bus grants since 2008 and encouraged growth of PTAs across Conroe ISD.

### 2B — Dyslexia Awareness Month Proclamation
- **Trustee Moore** read a proclamation he authored honoring **October as Dyslexia Awareness Month**.
- The proclamation recognized the gap between the estimated 20% of students who may be dyslexic and the approximately 3.5–4% identified statewide.
- It honored students, parents, educators, specialists, and researchers working with dyslexic students.
- Moore thanked district staff **Dr. Upshaw**, **Tracy Landis**, and **Dr. Henry**, as well as parents from **Better Education for Dyslexia** for keeping the issue in focus.
- The proclamation was to be signed by President Williams and placed in the official record and displayed at the district office.

### 2C — Barbara Robertson, RN — 2020 TSNO Administrator of the Year
- **Dr. Chris Heinz** recognized **Barbara Robertson**, Conroe ISD Coordinator of Health Services, as the **2020 Texas School Nurses Organization (TSNO) School Nurse Administrator of the Year**.
- Robertson has served as coordinator for six years and previously served 10 years as nurse at Caney Creek High School.
- Heinz highlighted her integral role in the district's COVID-19 pandemic response and her recent profile in **U.S. News & World Report**.
- Robertson thanked the board and district for supporting school health and recognized the work of all nurses and clinic assistants.

---

## Citizens Participation (Public Comment)

### Speaker 1 — Parent (Dyslexia Advocacy)
- Introduced her fourth-grade son **Devin**, a student in the dyslexia program at Bush Elementary.
- Thanked the board for recognizing Dyslexia Awareness Month and thanked district dyslexia staff.
- Presented **STAAR score data** from 2018–2019 highlighting campuses with below-state-average reading and writing scores across multiple grade levels.
- Argued that the district needs to do more for struggling readers, citing concerns about:
  - Fidelity of dyslexia program implementation (minimum time and maxed-out group sizes)
  - Under-identification of dyslexic students (3,125 identified out of 64,000 total students vs. estimated 20%)
  - Lack of dyslexia training for RTI and SPED teachers
  - Financial inequity — families without resources for private tutoring ($40–60/hour, 3–4 times/week) are disadvantaged
- Urged the board and Dr. Knoll to prioritize struggling readers.

### Speaker 2 — Justin Vaughn (Parent of Dyslexic Student)
- Shared his international experience with dyslexia education for his son James.
- Compared the comprehensive language intervention his son received overseas (full language arts block of 1.5–2 hours covering pronunciation, comprehension, fluency, and reading) to the **20-minute reading-only intervention** he observed in Conroe ISD.
- Made two requests:
  1. **Better parent education** — Give parents clear resources on what the school provides and what parents can do to support building blocks of literacy.
  2. **Transparency on funding** — Help parents understand how state dyslexia funding is allocated; advocated for **Certified Academic Language Therapists (CALTs)**.
- Acknowledged the addition of a fifth day of intervention compared to prior year as a positive step.

### Speaker 3 — Stuart Schroeder (Boy Scouts of America)
- Vice Chairman of the George Strait District, Sam Houston Area Council.
- Introduced **Carla Martinez**, the new district executive for scouting in the area.
- Highlighted Eagle Scout service projects in Conroe ISD (Oak tree planting at Oak Ridge HS, campus garden maintenance at Oak Ridge Elementary, lending library construction at Lamar Elementary).
- Noted COVID-19's impact on scouting activities and the transition to virtual programming.
- Requested continued district support for scouting partnerships.

### Speaker 4 — CISD Teacher (Job Description Concerns)
- Spoke on behalf of herself and fellow teachers who contacted her with concerns about a **new job description** sent out that teachers were expected to sign by Friday.
- Noted new terminology beyond what was described as simply adding online teaching language, including references to:
  - Standard and prolonged/irregular work hours
  - Communication during standard and prolonged work hours
  - Exposure to biological hazards
- Expressed frustration that this was being required **10 weeks into the school year** rather than having been addressed in May 2020 or at contract time.
- Said teachers feel **frustrated and demeaned**, and are already doing their best under difficult circumstances.

### Speaker 5 — Charles Bircher (Parent)
- Parent of a fourth grader and sixth grader; father is in hospice care at home.
- Expressed deep gratitude for the **virtual education program**, which allowed his children to continue learning safely without bringing potential exposure into their home.
- Praised the district for providing laptops within two days of his request and for the quality of online instruction.
- Raised two concerns:
  1. **Canvas errors** — 11 instances across both children in nine weeks where correct answers were marked incorrect in the Canvas system.
  2. **Transition issues** — His son's transition to new teachers in the second nine weeks resulted in less than an hour of instruction initially, with the new schedule showing only 1.5 hours/day total across all teachers.

### Speaker 6 — Steven Johnson (Parent)
- Parent of a 13-year-old at Peet Junior High.
- Argued against the district's **mask requirements for children under 10**, stating the governor's order does not require masks for that age group.
- Cited zero fatalities ages 0–20 in Montgomery County.
- Called mask requirements for face-to-face instruction "unconstitutional" and "separate and unequal."
- Urged the board to remove mask requirements and return to normal operations.

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 4)

**Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Public Hearing — District of Innovation Plan Renewal (Item 5)

### Presentation by Dr. Chris Heinz
- The district's **District of Innovation (DOI) designation** expires in January 2021 and must be renewed.
- Current plan only provides for a **flexible start date** (before the fourth Monday of August).
- A potential new addition to the plan may include a **waiver for CTE certifications** for hard-to-fill positions (welding, collision repair, automotive technology, health science, computer science).
- Outlined the timeline:
  - October 20: Public hearing, committee designation, resolution adoption
  - October 21: District Level Planning Committee begins developing plan
  - Plan posted on website for 30 days for public feedback
  - November 18: Committee reviews feedback
  - December 2: Plan finalized
  - December 15: Board vote on final plan

### Public Comment During Hearing
- A **TSTA Conroe representative (Carrie)** urged the board to maintain high teacher quality standards when considering CTE certification waivers and not to undermine teachers.

---

## Item 5B — Designation of DOI Plan Committee

- Board designated the **District Level Planning and Decision-Making Committee** as the District of Innovation Plan Committee (same committee used in 2015).
- Trustee Moore asked a governance question about why re-designation was necessary; Dr. Heinz explained the statute requires the full process to restart when a plan expires.
- **Trustee Hughman** expressed confidence in the process and in protecting teachers.

**Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 5C — Resolution to Renew District of Innovation Designation

**Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 6 — Teaching and Learning: Instructional Materials Selection Process (Information Item)

- **Dr. Upshaw** presented information about the state adoption of **pre-kindergarten instructional materials** in Spanish and English.
- A committee of campus representatives will evaluate materials and make a recommendation.
- A publisher showcase and community review period will be held at the instructional warehouse.
- Board recommendation expected in **March**.

**No vote required — information item only.**

---

## Item 7A — Guaranteed Maximum Price for New Teacher Training Center at Woodforest Stadium

- **Mr. Foster** presented the GMP of **$15,552,314** for the new Teacher Training Center.
- **Brookstone** is the construction manager at risk; **PBK** is the design firm.
- The building is approximately **49,000–50,000 square feet, two stories**, with multi-use/multipurpose ballroom space.
- Located on the **north end of Woodforest Stadium** property (opposite the natatorium).
- Board members noted the efficiency of the location — existing parking, central location for the district, reduced teacher travel.

**Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

| **Safety & Security 2020** (district-wide) | Wrapping up first responder radio systems; financial closeout pending | 2021 project to be bid in spring |

**No vote required — information item only.**

## Public Hearing — Financial Integrity Rating System of Texas (FIRST Report)

### Presentation by Karen Garza
- Conroe ISD received a **superior "A" rating** — **98 out of 100 points** (the state's highest rating).
- Based on 2018–19 school year data and fiscal year ending August 31, 2019.
- Key highlights:
  - **119 days cash on hand** (Indicator 6: 10/10)
  - Only deduction: **Indicator 7** (current assets to current liabilities) scored **8/10** due to $50 million strategically invested in longer-term instruments with TCG (ratio still a strong 2.53)
  - **Unmodified (clean) audit opinion**
  - Administrative cost ratio of **.0416** — less than half the state cap of .0855
  - All other indicators scored 10/10 or passed
  - Student enrollment increased by **3,073 students** from FY17–FY19
  - Indicator 5 not scored this year (TEA reviewing due to GASB 75/net pension liability)

### Public Comment
- No public comments received.

---

## Item 8B — Award RFP 20-08-06: New and Used Vehicles

- Recommended award to attached vendors for estimated annual expenditure of **$500,000**.
- Five vendors submitted bid responses.
- Evaluated by maintenance, transportation, technology, and CISD police departments.

**Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 8C — Award RFP 20-08-07: Auto Fleet Parts

- Recommended award for estimated annual expenditure of **$1.2 million**.
- 25 vendors submitted responses.
- One-year term with automatic annual renewal for two additional years (through October 2023).

**Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 8D — Final Results: Refunding and New Money Bond Series 2028

### Presentation by John Roebuck, BOK Financial Services
- Bonds sold on **October 6, 2020**, with closing scheduled for **November 17, 2020**.

**Refunding Portion:**
- Refunded **$199,630,000** in outstanding bonds
- Issued **$163,235,000** in new refunding bonds
- All-in true interest cost: **1.285%** (down from 4.478% on refunded bonds — a ~3.2% reduction)
- **Net present value savings: $50,067,485** (22.385% PV savings)
  - Annual savings of approximately **$3,575,000/year for 14 years**
- Roebuck noted that historically, 3% PV savings was considered good and 5% was great; 22.3% is extraordinary.

**New Money Portion:**
- Issued **$165,090,000** in bonds
- Generated **$189 million in project proceeds**
- All-in cost: **2.478%** (estimated had been ~4%)
- **$13 million less** in total interest costs than presented in July
- **$38 million less** in interest costs than estimated when the bond program went to voters in 2019

**Combined savings: approximately $88 million** versus original estimates.

- Board members praised the timing strategy — Roebuck and Mr. Rice waited several hours on the morning of the sale when rates ticked up slightly, which improved results significantly.

**No vote required — information/receive item.**
